questions.text: Arrange the fractions $\frac{5}{9}$, $\frac{4}{7}$, $\frac{3}{5}$, and $\frac{2}{3}$ in ascending order.[cite: 31]
Evaluate: $7\frac{1}{4} - \left[\frac{5}{6} \div \left\{\frac{1}{3} - \left(\frac{1}{2} \times \left(\frac{3}{4} - \frac{1}{4}\right)\right)\right\}\right]$[cite: 31]

Arvind started a business by investing ₹80,000. After 4 months, Bhavin joined with ₹1,20,000. At the end of 8 months from the start, Chandan joined with ₹1,60,000. If the total profit is ₹1,05,000 at the end of the year, find the share of Chandan.[cite: 31]
What is the average of all integers between 100 and 250 that are exactly divisible by 11?[cite: 31, 32]
A sum becomes ₹6,600 in 2 years and ₹7,920 in 3 years at compound interest. What is the original principal?[cite: 31, 32]

A toy manufacturer produced 1200 toy cars at a total cost of ₹90,000. He donated 200 cars to a charity event. For the rest, he announced a 10% discount on the market price of ₹120 per car. He also offered 2 toy cars free for every 8 toy cars purchased. If all 1200 toy cars were distributed, what is his overall gain or loss percentage?[cite: 31, 32]
A 40-liter mixture contains juice and water in the ratio 5:3. How much water (in liters) must be added to this mixture to change the ratio of juice to water to 2:3?[cite: 31, 32]
A is able to complete a task in 15 days, while B takes 20 days to finish the same task. If they collaborate and work together for 4 days, what fraction of the work will still remain?[cite: 31]

A bullet train covers a fixed distance in 30 minutes at an average speed of 240 km/h. Due to track maintenance, it needs to be diverted, increasing the distance by 20%. If the train needs to arrive at its destination on time (i.e., in 30 minutes), what should its new average speed be in km/h?[cite: 31, 32]
A ring-shaped disc has outer radius 10 cm and inner radius 7 cm. What is the approximate ratio of the ring's area to the whole outer circle?[cite: 31]
The line $y = mx + 5$ passes through (1,8). Find m.[cite: 31]
If $\tan(90^{\circ} - A) = \sqrt{3}$, what is $\sin A$?[cite: 31, 32]
If $x = \sqrt{7}$, determine the value of $x + \frac{1}{x}$.[cite: 31]
The angles of a cyclic quadrilateral are in the ratio 1:2:3:4. What is the measure of the smallest angle?[cite: 31, 32]
If 60% of A = 0.3 of B = $\frac{1}{6}$ of C, find A:B:C.[cite: 31]
Simplify: $\left(\frac{1}{7+\sqrt{2}}\right) + \left(\frac{1}{7-\sqrt{2}}\right) - \left(\frac{14}{49-2}\right)$[cite: 31]

The expenditure of a household on food, clothing, and entertainment is in the ratio 2:4:4. In the coming year, the cost of food is expected to fall by 10%, clothing to rise by 5%, and entertainment to fall by 15%. What is the percent change (increase or decrease) in the total household expenditure?[cite: 31]
The profit made on an item sold for ₹2200 is equal to the loss incurred when it is sold for ₹1800. What will be the profit or loss percentage if the item is sold for ₹2050?[cite: 31]
A right circular cone has a radius of 7 cm and height of 24 cm. What is the slant height?[cite: 31]

From a sample of students in two sections having different heights, Section A (< 1.65m) has 5 + 9 + 11 = 25 students. Section B (< 1.65m) has 4 + 8 + 13 = 25 students. What is the ratio of the total number of students in Section A whose height is less than 1.65 metres to the total number of students in Section B whose height is less than 1.65 metres?[cite: 31]
Two circular ponds have circumferences in the ratio 3:5. If the smaller pond has an area of 150 $m^2$, what is the approximate area of the larger pond?[cite: 31]
Given $x + \frac{1}{x} = 5$, determine the value of $x^2 + \frac{1}{x^2}$.[cite: 31]
A circular pond is surrounded by a path 2 m wide. If the total area (pond + path) is 616 $m^2$ and the pond area is 452.16 $m^2$, what is the radius of the pond?[cite: 31]
A car covers 40% of a certain distance at a pace of 20 km/h and the remaining distance at a pace of 30 km/h. What is the average speed of the car for the entire journey?[cite: 31]

One type of pulses is sold for ₹72 per kg at a 20% profit. Another type is sold for ₹60 per kg at a 25% profit. If these two varieties are mixed in the ratio of 2:1 (first variety to second variety) and the mixture is sold at ₹70 per kg, what is the overall profit or loss percentage?[cite: 31]
